refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation communication quality such that packet loss does not exceed the customer-defined limit under agreed network conditions.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (packet loss) and ties it explicitly to customer-defined limits and network conditions, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure packet loss percentage during teleoperation sessions under controlled network conditions and verify it remains within the customer-defined limit.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.
DP2: Use dedicated network link with QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP3: Real-time packet loss monitoring and dynamic adjustment of transmission parameters.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement adaptive communication protocol with error correction and retransmission.

potential_sources: Interaction between communication protocol and network link quality.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically isolates communication quality management, minimizing coupling with other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of increased latency due to retransmissions affecting teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring (DP3) as a complementary DP to enhance robustness.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation communication management, including protocol implementation and packet loss control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving teleoperation data packets over the network.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface to report communication quality metrics and packet loss status.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface to receive teleoperation commands and tasks.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the DP logic is fully encapsulated here; low diffusion since communication logic is centralized.
Moderate containment; some communication aspects may be shared but less focused.
Low containment; vehicle module mainly executes commands, not managing communication quality.
Minimal containment; safety services consume alerts but do not manage teleoperation communication.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the communication quality management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if Teleoperation module becomes overloaded with communication and control logic.
Dependency on Network Link interface reliability.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Monitor module load and consider offloading monitoring functions if diffusion risk increases.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP directly addresses the refined FR with clear mapping and measurable performance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk due to centralized DP implementation in Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support strong encapsulation of DP within Teleoperation IM.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by monitoring packet loss metrics via Telemetry interface during teleoperation tests.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider adding complementary DP for real-time monitoring to enhance robustness.
Evaluate potential latency impact due to retransmission strategies.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns a suitable IM with clear interfaces and containment. Verification and ownership are well defined.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor real-time packet loss during teleoperation.
FR2: Adapt communication parameters to maintain packet loss within limits.
FR3: Report packet loss status to relevant system components.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs if further detail is required.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Customer-defined packet loss limits are achievable with adaptive protocols.
open_risks: Latency impact from retransmission may affect teleoperation responsiveness.
Reliability of Network Link interface under varying conditions.
evidence_needed: Test data on packet loss under representative network conditions.
Performance metrics on latency and responsiveness during teleoperation.
